citation.description: Default `require_plan_review_for_close: bool = False` in VerifyConfig; gate_enabled returns False and close_plan_review_gate_check returns {ok:True, verdict:'disabled'} immediately (gates.py lines 70-77).

criteria.criterion: With `require_plan_review_for_close=false` or absent, normal close behavior is byte-for-byte equivalent and performs no plan-review reads.

citation.description: Plan gate runs at lines 554-567 (`if not force_close and ticket_type in _PLAN_REVIEW_CLOSE_TYPES`), then `_completion_precheck` at line 569—gate precedes completion check.

criteria.criterion: With enforcement enabled, task/story/epic close runs the plan gate before completion verification.

citation.description: `compute_validity` called with `profile=PlanValidityProfile.CLOSE` which disables code-freshness (HEAD/file-dep drift) but keeps other checks.

criteria.criterion: CLOSE accepts certified, registry-current, material-current, phase-compatible reviews despite HEAD or file-dependency drift.

citation.description: Parametrized test `test_close_gate_preserves_validity_failure_vocabulary` covers unsigned, stale-reopened, stale-regver, stale-material, unverifiable-material, malformed-pin, incompatible-phase, malformed-phase verdicts—all return ok=False.

criteria.criterion: Unsigned, reopened, stale-registry, own-material drift, malformed metadata, and phase incompatibility block even when pin enforcement is false.

citation.description: `enforce_plan_material_pins: bool = False` in VerifyConfig; `compute_validity` with PlanValidityProfile.CLOSE respects this flag for pin enforcement.

criteria.criterion: Pin drift/missing/malformed blocks only when `enforce_plan_material_pins=true`; disabled enforcement remains advisory.

citation.description: `_raise_plan_review_close_gate_error` produces `'plan-review close gate: <verdict>: <reason>. Run rebar review-plan <id> separately, then retry close.'`. Exception handling in gates.py lines 101-110 catches all exceptions and returns `{ok:False, verdict:'unavailable'}` with structured WARNING, no traceback propagation.

criteria.criterion: Every failure uses the exact verdict-prefixed message and separate `review-plan` remediation; unexpected exceptions fail closed as `unavailable` without traceback.

citation.description: `local_read_context()` context manager wraps all reads in `close_plan_review_gate_check`; no LLM, model, or network calls in the function; only local signature verification and validity computation.

criteria.criterion: Close performs zero plan-review LLM/model/network calls.

citation.description: Lock acquired at line 208, state re-read at 211, concurrency/structural checks at 230-265, then `pre_status_check(state)` at 296-297, then `finalize_event` and STATUS write at 302-309.

criteria.criterion: The generic `pre_status_check` runs under the existing lock after fresh state/structural checks and before STATUS append.

citation.description: At line 296-297 `pre_status_check` is called before `final_path` is assigned (line 305). On CommandError at 310-317: `final_path` is still None so no unstage/remove needed, and no STATUS file was written or git-added before the raise.

criteria.criterion: A failing locked callback appends and commits no STATUS event.

citation.description: `plan_review_recheck` closure calls `close_plan_review_gate_check(ticket_id, locked_state, ...)` with freshly reduced state inside the lock; a changed plan/sig/material raises CommandError before STATUS append.

criteria.criterion: A plan/signature/phase/material/pin change between precheck and lock is rejected by the locked recheck.

citation.description: `pre_status_check: Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], None] | None = None` — default is None; existing callers pass no argument and the `if pre_status_check is not None:` guard at line 296 skips invocation.

criteria.criterion: Existing transition callers that omit `pre_status_check` are unchanged.

citation.description: `_PLAN_REVIEW_CLOSE_TYPES = frozenset({'task','story','epic'})` — other types bypass; `current_status != 'idea'` guard at line 549 exempts idea→closed. `close_plan_review_gate_check` also checks ticket_type not in task/story/epic (line 78-79 in gates.py).

criteria.criterion: Bugs, session logs, code reviews, identities, and idea→closed retain their existing exemptions.

citation.description: Open-children guard at 514-532 is unconditional. Plan gate at line 554: `if not force_close and ticket_type in _PLAN_REVIEW_CLOSE_TYPES:` — force_close bypasses it. Completion precheck also bypasses on force_close (line 199 of _completion_precheck).

criteria.criterion: Non-empty force-close reason bypasses both gates with the existing audit trail but never bypasses structural child closure.

citation.description: '## Close-time attestation gate' section covers: CLOSE policy (lines 616-622), both flags `require_plan_review_for_close` and `enforce_plan_material_pins` (lines 610, 618), callback ordering (lines 624-629), error vocabulary and verdicts (lines 631-641), and bypass scope (lines 643-645).

criteria.criterion: `docs/plan-review-gate.md` documents the CLOSE policy, both flags, callback ordering, error vocabulary, and bypass scope.
